Name: Josh Allen
Outlook: He is him. While technically Allen finished with his lowest fantasy points per game average since 2019, he continued his streak of being the QB1 or QB2 for the sixth consecutive season. In 2024, Allen became the first top-5 fantasy QB without a top-36 scoring WR or top-12 TE. In 2025, he did it again. Same exact thing. Yes, he is now 30 years old, and the Bills mixed things up, firing HC Sean McDermott. The offense should remain the same, with the WR corps hopefully upgraded with the addition of DJ Moore. Allen is our unquestioned QB1 and the first pick in 2QB/SuperFlex leagues. However, in 1QB leagues, we caution taking him at the end of Round 2 and would prefer to load up on RBs or WRs.

Outlook: For the first three weeks of the season, it was vintage Lamar, averaging 27.6 fantasy points on the back of three passing TDs and 39 rushing yards per game.
